title: Product name: Decision Explorer AI
question: Should the public name add 'AI' - Decision Explorer to Decision Explorer AI?
rationale: Add 'AI' so the public name signals the tool is AI-native / Claude-driven, matching how it is actually used - same low-risk scope as the original rename (command, install path and folder untouched). The viewer title/brand/footer, generate.py embedded copy, SKILL.md, README, netlify.toml and _project.json all now say Decision Explorer AI.

options.label: Rebrand the product to 'Decision Explorer AI'

tradeoffs.note: the 'AI' suffix signals the tool is AI-native / Claude-driven, matching how it's actually used
viewer title, brand, footer and docs all updated together, so the app and product share one name
command, install path and folder untouched; only displayed name and docs change
already matches the viewer; nothing to re-touch
reads as a neutral name - doesn't signal the tool is AI-native the way 'AI' does
